behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: diff extraction -> review-input builder. A pure Unit test on one helper can miss wiring bugs where unchanged lines are reintroduced during assembly. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; fixture diff is enough. (b) no real AI/ML output dependency. (c) no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is about scoping content, not real API transport; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no model-quality decision is required.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate low (Unit): setup call `select_changed_lines(diff)` with synthetic patch; assertion only changed hunks returned; catches off-by-one hunk parsing bug; misses bug where caller later appends full file content. Candidate current (Integration): setup wire diff parser + prompt payload composer using fixture PR file metadata and patch; assertion final review payload contains only changed-line snippets; catches cross-module merge bug that leaks unchanged lines; misses GitHub API auth/pagination failures. Candidate high (System): setup real PR fetch then inspect outgoing payload; assertion unchanged lines absent; catches real API shape drift; misses little beyond integration for this behavior. Choose Integration as minimum that catches stated failure mode.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real GitHub diff-position semantics (commit SHA, path, side/line mapping) that mocks often simplify. MOCK-HIDE risk is high: mocked API can accept invalid positions that real API rejects or reanchors incorrectly. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because this behavior is about API coordinate mapping, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate low (Integration): setup mocked GitHub client validates requested position fields; assertion mapper emits expected `path/line/side`; catches local mapping arithmetic bug; misses server-side interpretation differences. Candidate current (System): setup real test PR with known diff anchors; post inline comments via real API; assertion comments appear at intended lines in PR diff view; catches wrong `line` vs `position` usage and SHA mismatch failures; misses model-quality issues (out of scope). Candidate high (Agentic): add real LLM-generated findings before posting; catches extra analysis failures; misses none for placement but is above required boundary. Choose System.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on real model judgment quality (detecting an actual defect), which is non-deterministic and cannot be validated with fixed stubs. Deterministic-assertion trap applies: a stubbed model always passes/fails by fixture, hiding whether the model can truly find the bug. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior requires one model decision outcome (find >=1 real defect), not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate low (Integration): setup stub LLM returns one canned bug finding; assertion pipeline records at least one finding; catches parsing/serialization bugs; misses true detection failure. Candidate current (Agentic): setup known-bug PR diff corpus, call real model repeatedly with fixed prompt; assertion at least one finding matches seeded defect rubric; catches model/prompt regressions where real defect is missed; misses downstream posting failures. Candidate high (Workflow): include full posting pipeline plus analysis; catches orchestration failures too; misses little but exceeds minimum for this specific behavior. Choose Agentic.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: This is run-local consistency across modules: inline posting results -> summary assembler. Real failure is state handoff/ID join logic, not external infra. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess; fake poster responses with IDs suffice. (b) no AI output quality dependency. (c) no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because real API not required to catch ID-mismatch bug; Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior is deterministic referential integrity.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate low (Unit): setup call `build_summary(findings, inline_ids)` directly; assertion each summary item has matching id; catches local filtering bug; misses mismatch introduced between posting module and aggregator. Candidate current (Integration): setup execute posting-result collector + summary builder with simulated same-run and prior-run IDs; assertion summary references only IDs returned in current run; catches cross-module stale-ID join bug; misses real API outages. Candidate high (System): setup real PR post then summarize; assertion IDs map to created comments; catches integration with live API id format drift; misses little extra for this behavior. Choose Integration.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets completeness across pipeline stages (finding list -> posted inline map -> summary payload). Drops often occur at module boundaries (dedupe/filter/serialization), so Unit is too narrow. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess for detecting drop logic. (b) no dependence on model output quality when using fixed findings. (c) no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because payload completeness bug is catchable before live post; Agentic/Workflow rejected because this is deterministic dataflow integrity.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate low (Unit): setup run `summarize(findings)` with list of findings; assertion count preserved; catches local loop/condition bug; misses drops happening between poster response normalization and summary input. Candidate current (Integration): setup wire normalizer + deduper + summary payload builder with fixed multi-finding set (including edge duplicates); assertion every posted inline finding id appears in summary payload exactly once per rule; catches boundary bug where one finding is lost during transformation; misses network/API failures. Candidate high (System): setup real posting and summary on test PR; assertion no missing finding in final posted summary; catches live transport truncation; misses little beyond integration for stated failure mode. Choose Integration.

behaviors.description: The automation sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real pull request when provided a fixed precomputed finding set.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end success on a real PR, including real HTTP/API and ordering of side effects. Mocks can hide auth, permission, rate-limit, and API contract issues. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected by concrete text "fixed precomputed finding set" (no model-output correctness dependency). Workflow rejected because no multiple agentic steps are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate low (Integration): setup fake PR service + fake comment poster with fixed findings; assertion call order and success status; catches orchestration ordering bug; misses real GitHub API failures/permissions. Candidate current (System): setup disposable real PR, inject fixed findings, execute read->inline post->summary post against real API; assertion all steps complete and artifacts exist; catches real token-scope, SHA mismatch, and endpoint contract bugs; misses model-quality issues (out of scope). Candidate high (Workflow): same with live model analysis before posting; catches additional agentic failures but exceeds stated behavior. Choose System.

behaviors.description: The full live run with model-generated findings (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is a full agent pipeline combining real infrastructure and model-generated findings, with correctness depending on sequential handoff from model output to posting and final summary. MULTI-STEP applies at pipeline level: analysis decision plus downstream automated actions must succeed together without human repair. REJECTION-GATE: lower levels disqualified by behavior text "full live run" and "model-generated findings" + real PR requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate low (Agentic): setup real model on known PR diff but stop before posting; assertion findings generated; catches analysis regressions; misses failures in comment placement/summary orchestration. Candidate current (Workflow): setup real PR, run full unattended pipeline end-to-end with real model and real API; assertion run exits success, inline comments posted, summary posted, and summary references posted inline items; catches cross-stage failures where valid findings fail to propagate or post. Candidate high (Workflow with stress): repeat across multiple PR shapes/concurrency; catches flakiness/rate-limit resilience but is beyond minimum for single behavior acceptance. Choose Workflow.
